steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the two rivers. The notes establish that the Muskwa River has high sinuosity and the Cheyyar River has low sinuosity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Muskwa River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Cheyyar River.: Directly compares the sinuosity of both rivers, using the correct data from the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — By comparing the stream lengths of the Muskwa and Cheyyar rivers to their valley lengths, one can calculate their curviness, or sinuosity.: Explains the calculation method but does not state which river is curvier. ✗
  • Choice C — In both the Muskwa and the Cheyyar rivers, curviness is determined by comparing stream length to valley length.: Also describes the method, not a comparison of the two rivers’ actual curviness. ✗
  • Choice D — The high-sinuosity Cheyyar River in Tamil Nadu is curvier than the low-sinuosity Muskwa River in British Columbia.: Reverses the data — the notes say the Muskwa has high sinuosity and the Cheyyar has low sinuosity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
